title: calendula officinalis
intro: Calendula officinalis, commonly known as pot marigold, is a flowering plant belonging to the daisy family. This plant is valued for its numerous health benefits, including its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The therapeutic actions of calendula officinalis include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its effectiveness in treating various skin conditions and injuries. The bioactive constituents of calendula officinalis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
actions: Calendula officinalis has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, antifungal, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in wound healing, skin regeneration, and soothing irritated tissues. Its therapeutic actions also include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipruritic properties, which can reduce redness, swelling, and itching caused by skin conditions like eczema, acne, and rosacea. Additionally, calendula officinalis has been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, which can boost the body's natural defense mechanisms and improve overall health and well-being. By harnessing these therapeutic actions, people can benefit from improved skin health, reduced pain and inflammation, and enhanced quality of life, enabling them to engage in daily activities with greater comfort and confidence.
intro_study: According to "BioMed research international", calendula officinalis hydroalcoholic extract may improve oxidative stress, restore folliculogenesis, and increase ovulation in rats with polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) and nonovulation.
benefits: Calendula officinalis has many benefits, such as being a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, making it effective in treating wounds, cuts, and burns, as well as soothing skin irritations like eczema, acne, and rosacea. Its antimicrobial properties also help to combat infections and promote healing, which can significantly improve the quality of life for individuals with chronic health conditions or compromised immune systems. Additionally, the plant's antioxidant and anti-aging properties can reduce fine lines and wrinkles, improve skin elasticity, and even help to prevent age-related diseases like cancer and Alzheimer's. By harnessing the power of calendula officinalis, individuals can enjoy improved physical and mental health, increased energy levels, and a more radiant, youthful appearance.
constituents: Calendula officinalis has many active constituents, such as triterpene saponins, flavonoids, phenolic acids, carotenoids, and volatile oils, which provide a range of health benefits. These constituents, including apigenin, luteolin, and quercetin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe skin irritations, reduce redness and swelling, and protect against cell damage. Additionally, the plant's sesquiterpene lactones, such as calendulin and calendulol, possess antimicrobial properties that can aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infection. By harnessing the medicinal constituents of Calendula officinalis, people can experience improved skin health, reduced inflammation, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health concerns.
parts: Calendula officinalis has many medicinal parts, such as its flowers, leaves, and roots. The flowers, which are often used in herbal remedies, contain medicinal constituents like triterpene saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids, which possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves and stems of the plant contain essential oils, including calendol and sesquiterpenes, which have antiseptic and antifungal properties. The roots and rhizomes of the plant contain sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
preparations: Calendula officinalis has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, salves, and infusions, which are used for various medicinal purposes. Calendula tea is often used to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation, while calendula tincture can be applied topically to treat skin conditions like eczema and acne. Calendula salve, on the other hand, is commonly used to moisturize and protect dry skin, wounds, and burns, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of infection. Additionally, calendula infusions can be used as a compress to relieve menstrual cramps and other types of pain.
side_effects: Calendula officinalis can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing skin irritation, allergic reactions, or even anaphylaxis in rare cases, especially when applied undiluted or in large quantities. Prolonged use of calendula tea or extract can lead to gastrointestinal issues like stomach upset, diarrhea, or even interact with certain medications. Excessive consumption of calendula may also cause liver damage or worsen pre-existing liver conditions due to its high levels of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Moreover, calendula oil can cause skin dryness, redness, or itchiness if not properly diluted or used in combination with other irritant essential oils.
